title: The datey specification
text: datey system enables high performance consistent fixed precision date duration arithmetic annual grid across multiple platforms. Replicating real-world date arithmetic text representations non goal. Handling complexity take place outside datey system. typical use, dates inputs datey system, outputs (durations neither1). datey approach assume input dates calculated appropriately job handle intervals dates. note uses YYYY‑MM‑DD notation dates, YYYY four-digit year, MM two-digit month year, 01 12, DD two-digit day month, 01 31.

title: The datey specification
text: datey system assumes following: calendar years duration. Within calendar year, days duration. Time granular, smallest indivisible unit click, 1 / 534 360 year. variation arising , instance, time zones, daylight saving time leap seconds scope – allowance must done outside datey system.

title: The datey specification
text: datey system defines two types: datey represents date. durationy represents difference two dates. types represented using clicks stored 32-bit two’s complement integers.

title: The datey specification
text: Dates mapped dateys number clicks since start notional year 0000 proleptic Gregorian calendar, .e. Gregorian calendar extended backwards introduction 1582. datey otherwise represent date calendar year 1000 calendar year 2999 treated invalid2 mapping dates datey.

what: Mapping a YMD date to a datey
title: The datey specification
text: mapping date specified calendar year, month day datey point within day also needs specified. typical use, expected start, middle end day specified, mapping include day-fraction provided full generality. mapping clicks function datey_from_YMDF(year, month, day, day_fraction), defined follows year, month, day day_fraction invalid result invalid. Valid inputs year: integer 1000 2999 inclusive month: integer 1 12 inclusive day: integer 1 number days month specified year month day_fraction: double precision float point number 0 1 inclusive addition, special case 0999-12-31 day_fraction precisely equal 1 also treated valid. Define clicks_per_day 1460 year leap year 1464 otherwise. Define integer days_to_start_of_month number days start year start month (allowing whether year leap year). Define integer day_count days_to_start_of_month + day − 1. Define integer fraction_clicks round(day_fraction × clicks_per_day), round means round integer using banker’s rounding3. resulting number clicks defined integer calculation year × 534 360 + day_count × clicks_per_day + fraction_clicks avoidance doubt, special case 2999-12-31 day_fraction rounds 1 returned . convenience, datey implementation also provide mapping datey start, middle end date using follows. start_day(year, month, day) := datey_from_YMDF(year, month, day, 0.0) mid_day(year, month, day) := datey_from_YMDF(year, month, day, 0.5) end_day(year, month, day) := datey_from_YMDF(year, month, day, 1.0) Worked examples:

what: Mapping a datey to a YMDF date
title: The datey specification
text: datey mapped clicks year, month, day day_fraction follows: clicks < 1000 × 534 360 clicks ≥ 3000 × 534 360 datey invalid therefore result also invalid. year clicks div 534 360, div means integer division rounded nearest integer. Define clicks_remaining clicks − year × 534 360. Define clicks_per_day 1460 year leap year 1464 otherwise. Define day_in_year clicks_remaining div clicks_per_day, div means integer division rounded nearest integer. Use day_in_year determine month day. Define day_fraction_clicks clicks_remaining − day_in_year × clicks_per_day. day_fraction day_fraction_clicks / clicks_per_day (using double precision division).

title: The datey specification
text: durationy represents duration using clicks. durationy otherwise represent duration magnitude 2000 years treated invalid mapping durations durationy.

what: Mapping a duration in years to a durationy
title: The datey specification
text: well difference two dateys, durationy can also defined direct duration years follows: duration years represented numerical type double precision floating point must first converted double precision floating point. entails loss precision error. duration years number (NaN) greater magnitude 2000 resulting durationy invalid. Otherwise resulting durationy number clicks duration years multiplied 534 360 rounded using banker’s rounding4. Worked examples:

what: Mapping a durationy to a duration in years
title: The datey specification
text: durationy mapped duration years double precision floating point number follows: durationy invalid result number (NaN). Otherwise result clicks converted double precision floating point divided 534 360.

title: The datey specification
text: following arithmetic operations defined applying specified operators underlying argument clicks using 32-bit two’s complement arithmetic. unary plus (+) minus (negation −) operators defined durationy: + operator returns argument unchanged. − operator changes sign clicks. required ot expected operations checked integer overflow – can reasonably supposed safe within domain operation (.e. date inputs already checked validity) datey exists facilitate performant calculations. Duration scaling, .e. number × durationy, durationy × number durationy / number, defined Scaling factors (.e. ‘number’ ) represented numerical types double precision floating point must first converted double precision floating point. entails loss precision error. durationy invalid result invalid. Otherwise convert underlying clicks double precision floating point, carry specified floating point calculation convert resulting floating point clicks back durationy follows: floating point clicks number (NaN) absolute value greater 2000 × 534 360 result invalid. Otherwise result floating point clicks rounded integer using banker’s rounding5. contrast operations involving solely dateys durationys, scaling operations checked validity result (consequently used hot paths).

title: The datey specification
text: datey system provides simple, standardised text input output format. complicated scenarios, dates parsed formatted outside datey system.

title: The datey specification
text: valid datey represented text format YYYY‑MM‑DD.FFF YYYY four-digit year, MM two-digit month year (starting “01” January) DD two-digit day month (first day month “01”), .FFF optional day fraction (‘.’ present least 1 digit must also provided). text outputs: fraction contains 3 decimal places (sufficient distinguish possible day fractions datey precision). option whether zero day fraction stated explicitly (e.g. ‘2000-01-01.0’). text inputs: Blank fractions treated zero. fractional part constrained 3 decimal places. Arbitrarily long inputs e.g. 100 UTF-8 bytes rejected.

title: The datey specification
text: Unless durationy serialised, recommended durations parsed formatted using conventional means applied duration measured years. valid durationy represented text format SY.FFFFFF UUU S plus minus sign, .e. one ‘+’ (U+002B), true minus ‘−’ (U+2212) ASCII hyphen-minus ‘-’ (U+002D). Y number whole years .FFFFFF optional fractional part year. UUU unit name one year. UUU non-blank preceded space. text outputs: optional whether ‘+’ (U+002B) plus sign included positive durations optional. (Default ‘+’). optional whether use true minus sign ‘−’ (U+002B) ASCII ‘-’ (U+002D) hyphen-minus character. (Default true minus sign.) fraction contains 6 decimal places (sufficient distinguish possible duration fractions datey precision). unit user-specifiable. (Default ‘yrs’.) text inputs: plus minus sign included parsed (including zero durations). fractional part constrained 6 decimal places. unit user-specifiable. non-blank unit text must present preceded space. (default ’ yrs’.) Arbitrarily long inputs e.g. 100 UTF-8 bytes rejected.

what: Annualised Fixed-Precision Dates and Durations for Actuarial Experience Analysis
title: Annualised Fixed-Precision Dates and Durations for Actuarial Experience Analysis
text: datey package provides standard mapping dates onto annual grid. matters contexts primary unit years definitions need precise. classic example actuarial mortality experience analysis valuation life assurance annuities. Mortality rates defined per annum experience valuation data usually defined using dates (.e. days). benefits using datey : consistent framework converting dates uniform annual grid. Handling often-overlooked issue whether date means start, end day. Fixed precision arithmetic, excludes bugs relating floating point arithmetic1. Long story short: working primarily annual rates annual domain data specifies time using dates, worth considering using datey.

title: Annualised Fixed-Precision Dates and Durations for Actuarial Experience Analysis
text: can install development version datey GitHub :
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("logmu-org/r-datey")

what: Convert a datey to calendar years (including fractional part) — as_years_datey
title: Convert a datey to calendar years (including fractional part) — as_years_datey
text: Converts datey calendar years. fractional part represents proportion calendar year elapsed. example, start 2000-01-01 (, equivalently, end 1999-12-31), results 2000, middle calendar year 2000 results 2000.5. Note following: .numeric() .double(). .integer() gives calendar year, e.g. .integer(datey(2000.9)) 2000. .integer(x) .integer(.double(x)).

what: Create a datey from a calendar year (including its fractional part) or another date type — datey
title: Create a datey from a calendar year (including its fractional part) or another date type — datey
text: package provides methods create datey following: double integer interpreted specified calendar year, fractional part representing fraction year. instance, datey(2000.5) means halfway though year 2000. (means integer argument always indicates start calendar year, e.g. datey(2000L) start year 2000.) Date POSIXct POSIXlt interpreted fractional years. day_fraction argument provided day fraction determined hours, minutes, seconds. instance, datey(.POSIXct("2000-03-21 12:00")) means middle 2000-03-21. Note standard use, Date fractional part therefore means start day. instance, datey(.Date("2000-03-21 12:00")) means start 2000-03-21. character – day_fraction provided text format must ISO 8601 extended format, .e. YYYY-MM-DD. day_fraction provided text format must YYYY-MM-DD.FFF, .FFF day fraction must present even fraction 0, e.g. "2000-01-01.0" start 1 January 2000. datey interpreted optional day_fraction override. Note day_fraction 1 add day day boundary, even originally defined end day. lengths vector arguments must multiples . S3 generic.

title: Create a datey from a calendar year (including its fractional part) or another date type — datey
code: datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, strict = TRUE, ...)  # Default S3 method datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, strict = TRUE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'datey' datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, strict = TRUE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'integer' datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, strict = TRUE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'double' datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, strict = TRUE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'Date' datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, strict = TRUE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'POSIXct' datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, strict = TRUE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'POSIXlt' datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, strict = TRUE, ...)

title: Create a datey from a calendar year (including its fractional part) or another date type — datey
text: x argument convert datey. day_fraction day_fraction override. Defaults NULL. day_fraction provided x used derive calendar year, month, day day fraction. day_fraction provided x used solely derive calendar year, month day, day_fraction provides position day. day_fraction must lie inclusive interval [0,1], 0 meaning start day, 0.5 meaning middle day, 1 meaning end day (identical start next day). strict calendar years less 1000 greater equal 3000 day fractions interval [0,1] handled. strict TRUE – default – execution stopped. strict FALSE NA returned. (NAs result NA regardless switch.) ... arguments (used package).

what: Whether datey and durationy are NA — is_NA
title: Whether datey and durationy are NA — is_NA
text: Valid datey system ranges: Valid dates calendar years interval [1000, 3000). Valid durations magnitude 2000 years less. Values outside ranges treated NA. .na() tests whether datey durationy NA element. anyNA() tests whether element datey durationy NA. convenience, constants NA_datey_ NA_datey_ datey durationy versions NA respectively, integer constants describing valid ranges also provided. performance reasons, intermediate calculations may check NAs.

title: Whether datey and durationy are NA — is_NA
code: # S3 method for class 'datey' is.na(x)  # S3 method for class 'datey' anyNA(x, recursive = FALSE)  # S3 method for class 'durationy' is.na(x)  # S3 method for class 'durationy' anyNA(x, recursive = FALSE)

title: Whether datey and durationy are NA — is_NA
text: x datey durationy test NA recursive Currently required FALSE (default)

title: Whether datey and durationy are NA — is_NA
text: .na() returns vector logical length x. anyNA() always returns TRUE FALSE, never NA never anything single value.

title: Whether datey and durationy are NA — is_NA
code: t <- c(NA_datey_, datey(2000))   is.na(t) # c(TRUE, FALSE) #> [1]  TRUE FALSE   anyNA(t) # TRUE #> [1] TRUE    d <- c(NA_durationy_, durationy(1.5))   is.na(d) # c(TRUE, FALSE) #> [1]  TRUE FALSE   anyNA(d) # TRUE #> [1] TRUE

text: x durationy print format. include_plus Whether include plus ('+') sign positive durations. Defaults FALSE. use_true_minus Whether use true minus sign ('−', U+2212) sign opposed ASCII hyphen (-, U+2212). Defaults FALSE. year_unit year unit name print. blank value followed space unit. 20 characters (UTF-8 bytes) contain control characters. Defaults "yr". ... arguments passed methods. max Numeric NULL, specifying maximal number entries printed. NULL, getOption("max.print") used. Defaults NULL.

title: Format or print a durationy — text_from_durationy
code: pos <- durationy(1)   neg <- durationy(-2.3)   format(pos) # "1 yr" #> [1] "1 yr"   format(pos, include_plus = TRUE) # "1 yr" #> [1] "+1 yr"   format(pos, year_unit = "") # "1" #> [1] "1"   format(neg) # "−2.3 yr" #> [1] "−2.3 yr"   format(neg, use_true_minus = TRUE) # "−2.3 yr" #> [1] "−2.3 yr"   format(neg, year_unit = "a") # "-2.3 a" #> [1] "−2.3 a"

what: Create a datey for the start, middle or end of a day — xxx_day
title: Create a datey for the start, middle or end of a day — xxx_day
text: Create datey start, middle end day specified calendar year, month day. lengths vector arguments must multiples . deconstruct datey, use to_ymdf().

title: Create a datey for the start, middle or end of a day — xxx_day
code: start_day(year, month, day, strict = TRUE)  mid_day(year, month, day, strict = TRUE)  end_day(year, month, day, strict = TRUE)  as_start_day(x, ...)  as_mid_day(x, ...)  as_end_day(x, ...)

title: Create a datey for the start, middle or end of a day — xxx_day
text: year Calendar year. Valid years 1000 (inclusive) 3000 (exclusive). provided double must integers. month Month number calendar year, 1 representing January. provided double must integers. day Day number month, 1 representing first day month. provided double must integers. strict handle calendar years less 1000 greater equal 3000 day fractions interval [0,1]. strict TRUE – default – execution stopped. strict FALSE NA returned. (NA arguments result NA regardless strict.) day_fraction fraction day, [0,1]. 0 means start day, 0.5 means middle day, 1 means end day (identical start next day).

title: Create a datey for the start, middle or end of a day — xxx_day
text: following special cases: end_day(999, 12, 31) cause error, even though theory represents legal datey 1000-01-01.0. end_day(2999, 12, 31) cause error, even though theory represents illegal datey 3000-01-01.0. resulting datey invalid though.

what: datey in terms of calendar year, month, day and day fraction components — ymdf
title: datey in terms of calendar year, month, day and day fraction components — ymdf
text: to_ymdf() returns list year, month, day day_fraction breakdown datey, year integer [1000,3000), month integer [1,12], day integer [1,N], N number days month specified year month, day_fraction double [0,1) representing fraction day, e.g. 0 means start 0.5 means middle day. datey constructed using end_day day_fraction = 1 to_ymdf() return start next day day_fraction = 0. from_ymdf() creates datey calendar year, month, day fraction. lengths vector arguments must multiples .

title: datey in terms of calendar year, month, day and day fraction components — ymdf
code: to_ymdf(datey)  point_in_day(year, month, day, day_fraction, strict = TRUE)

title: datey in terms of calendar year, month, day and day fraction components — ymdf
text: datey datey deconstructed from_ymdf(). year Calendar year. Valid years 1000 (inclusive) 3000 (exclusive). provided double must integers. month Month number calendar year, 1 representing January. provided double must integers. day Day number month, 1 representing first day month. provided double must integers. day_fraction fraction day, [0,1]. 0 means start day, 0.5 means middle day, 1 means end day (identical start next day). strict handle calendar years less 1000 greater equal 3000 day fractions interval [0,1]. strict TRUE – default – execution stopped. strict FALSE NA returned. (NA arguments result NA regardless strict.)

title: datey in terms of calendar year, month, day and day fraction components — ymdf
text: following special cases: from_ymdf(999, 12, 31, 1) cause error, even though theory represents legal datey 1000-01-01.0. from_ymdf(2999, 12, 31, 1) cause error, even though theory represents illegal datey 3000-01-01.0. resulting datey invalid though.

title: datey (development version)
text: Development version

title: datey 0.0.1
